scoring_window: weekly (heartbeat + standup + reaper) — aggregated across 7 days of runs
notes: Rubric for the Nehemiah conductor agent (netlify/functions/nehemiah-conductor.js). Evaluates the orchestrator itself: heartbeat reliability, queue health, escalation correctness, standup quality.

heartbeat_reliability.description: nehemiah.heartbeat event emitted every 15 min (96 beats/day target). Misses logged. Dashboard read latency < 30 sec after beat emitted.

scoring.90_100: ≥ 94 of 96 beats/day emitted on time. Zero silent misses. Dashboard reads current heartbeat within 30 sec.
scoring.70_89: 90-93 of 96 beats/day. Any miss logged with cause.
scoring.50_69: 85-89 of 96 beats/day OR a miss stretch > 2 consecutive ticks without a logged cause.
scoring.0_49: < 85 beats/day OR the dashboard went > 30 min without a fresh beat.

heartbeat_reliability.evidence_required: Heartbeat event log (timestamps) for the evaluation window + dashboard read-latency sample

queue_health.description: Reaper sweeps stale claims (> SLA per agent type) and correctly re-queues or dead-letters. Queue depth stays within budget (no runaway backlog). Idempotency holds (no double-processing of reaped claims).

scoring.90_100: Zero claims stuck > 2× SLA. Reaper decisions audit-clean. Queue depth bounded throughout window.
scoring.70_89: ≤ 2 claims stuck > 2× SLA (caught and resolved within the window).
scoring.50_69: A runaway backlog event (queue depth > 50) that took > 1 hour to resolve.
scoring.0_49: A double-processed claim OR a dead-lettered claim that should have been re-queued (lost work).

queue_health.evidence_required: agent-queue.json snapshots (start + end of window) + reaper decision log

escalation_correctness.description: Red-tier events surface to Marc immediately (defined list: Abigail hallucination, Benaiah CASL fail, Lazarus pitch-on-call-1, Levite vault alert, Daniel empty brief, any agent paused). Yellow-tier events aggregate into the 8am standup. No red escalated as yellow. No yellow escalated as red (cry-wolf).

scoring.90_100: Every red-tier event in the window reached Marc within 5 min. Every yellow-tier event landed in the standup. Zero mis-tier.
scoring.70_89: One mis-tier event (red sent as yellow OR yellow sent as red) — self-corrected.
scoring.50_69: Two or more mis-tier events OR a red-tier event reached Marc > 30 min late.
scoring.0_49: A red-tier event was silently swallowed (never reached Marc) OR cry-wolf spam (≥ 5 false-red alerts in 24h).

escalation_correctness.evidence_required: Event log filtered by tier + Marc email/SMS delivery timestamps + standup content diff

standup_quality.description: Daily standup at 8:00 AM ET, every day (7/week), ≤ 200 words, BLUF, named-agent granularity, includes pattern-calls not just individual failures. Cross-cut insight (e.g., Apollos + Lydia scoring down together → brief-format issue) present when data warrants.

scoring.90_100: 7/7 standups delivered on time. ≤ 200 words. Every agent named. At least one cross-cut insight per week when data warrants.
scoring.70_89: 6 of 7 standups delivered (one missed or > 30 min late). Named-agent granularity held.
scoring.50_69: ≤ 5 of 7 standups delivered OR standup exceeded 300 words OR cross-cut insight missing when data clearly warranted.
scoring.0_49: ≤ 3 standups/week OR a standup that said 'most agents ran fine' without named granularity.

standup_quality.evidence_required: Copy of each standup in the window + delivery timestamps + samples of cross-cut insight lines (or logged absence)

ground_truth_signals: Marc's 'did the standup surface the right thing?' rating (sampled weekly, 1-5)
